title: "You'll need journalism so distinctive it has its own gravity": NYT publisher A.G. Sulzberger on how news organizations can stand up to AI companies
tldr: Sulzberger's WAN-IFRA keynote argues news orgs must build journalism so distinctive AI can't replicate it, and stop being passive in the face of AI abuses. A foundational think-piece on media's existential AI moment.
why_fresh: high-profile strategic framing of AI threat to journalism
angle: What 'gravity' means for brand content: building distinctiveness AI can't summarize away.

title: 'Social media is now three distinct categories'
tldr: Researchers argue platforms have split into broadcasters, micro-communities, and AI-driven experiences — a structural reframing of how audiences actually use social. Reshapes social strategy thinking.
why_fresh: new conceptual model for social platforms
angle: How to build a content strategy for the three-platform reality: broadcast, micro-community, and AI.

title: The #1 Role Your Content Team Needs in 2026 Is a Managing Editor
tldr: As AI makes content cheap, editorial judgment becomes the differentiator — Contently argues every content team needs a managing editor. A clear ops-level response to the AI content glut.
why_fresh: reframes team roles for AI era
angle: Why editorial judgment is the new scarce resource — and how to structure a team around it.

title: Your Content Isn't Just Competing With Other Brands Anymore
tldr: In AI-driven discovery, your content competes with AI summaries and answer engines, not just competitor brands — breaking the old SEO/share-of-voice model. Essential strategic shift to grasp.
why_fresh: redefines the competitive set for content
angle: How to write for a world where the AI summary is your real competitor.

title: How to prepare for more declines in traditional Google search traffic
tldr: With Google's AI answers cutting clicks, experts share tactics for maintaining visibility when AI supplies the answers. Practical guide to the zero-click reality.
why_fresh: ongoing AI search shift, practical not novel
angle: A content visibility playbook for the post-search-traffic era.

title: Coca-Cola's AI-powered José Mourinho campaign could signal a shift in celebrity partnerships
tldr: Coke's series is hosted by Mourinho's AI clone instead of the man himself, hinting at a new model for synthetic celebrity endorsements. A concrete example of synthetic media going mainstream.
angle: When the AI clone hosts the campaign: what synthetic endorsements mean for brand trust.

title: YouTube's AI remix push exposes a looming reckoning for the creator economy
tldr: YouTube's Gemini Omni integration surfaces the thorny problems generative AI poses for creators and rights. A signal of the next creator-economy conflict.
angle: The coming fight over who owns AI-remixed creator content.

title: A viral space moment fell in Nutella's lap. Here's how they capitalized.
tldr: When a Nutella jar floated past astronauts on the Artemis II broadcast, the brand reacted with speed and restraint. A clean case study in real-time newsjacking done right.
angle: The discipline of stepping out of the spotlight when a viral moment lands in your lap.

title: Layoffs don't have to feel inhumane
tldr: Argues layoffs are a trust test, not a messaging problem, and that employees judge leadership almost instantly in 2026. Sharp reframe for internal comms and change management.
angle: Layoffs as the ultimate internal-comms credibility test.

title: 3 questions to ask before putting your CEO in front of the media during a crisis
tldr: FleishmanHillard's Elizabeth Cook warns a crisis isn't the time to coach a CEO on their weaknesses. Practical executive-positioning and crisis-comms guidance.
angle: How to honestly assess whether your CEO should face the media in a crisis.

title: Podcasts and influencers least culturally relevant media
tldr: Thinkbox study finds TV holds the largest share of media's cultural influence while podcasts and creators rank lowest — a contrarian data point against creator-economy hype.
angle: The creator-economy reality check: cultural relevance vs. attention.

title: Манифест для креаторов: репортаж с Shorty Awards
tldr: SETTERS report from the Shorty Awards on the end of big brands', big budgets', and glossy production's monopoly over creative. A view on where creator-led content power is shifting.
why_fresh: signals shift in creative power away from big brands
angle: Why scrappy creator work is beating glossy brand production — and what it means for content teams.

title: The Future of Content Belongs to the Tastemakers
tldr: With AI making polished copy trivial, taste and curation become the differentiator that makes content memorable. A craft-and-strategy argument for the AI age.
why_fresh: taste as the new content moat
angle: Taste as competitive advantage when everyone can generate competent content.

title: Understanding the 3 categories of online chatter
tldr: Ragan session on how news now emerges bottom-up from comment sections and social posts rather than top-down from gatekeepers, and how to monitor it. Useful framework for issue management.
angle: Mapping the three layers of online chatter for early issue detection.

title: Why "AI Productivity Gains" Is the Wrong Pitch for Every Stakeholder Above You
tldr: Argues 'AI productivity gains' won't move your CMO, CFO, or legal — reframe the metric per audience to defend headcount and unlock budget. Smart internal-selling and data-storytelling advice.
why_fresh: fresh angle on framing AI ROI internally
angle: How to translate AI value into the language each executive stakeholder actually cares about.
